fact: August 14 is exactly 226 days into the year, which means there are only 139 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is prime time for cicadas to be screaming their loudest - some can buzz as loud as a motorcycle!
The sun rises about 2 minutes later than it did just a week ago, and summer is secretly starting to sneak away.
August 14 falls smack in the middle of Leo season, when the zodiac lion is roaring at full strength until August 22.
Monarch butterflies in North America are getting ready for their epic 3,000-mile journey south - some might start fluttering toward Mexico any day now!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, August 14 is deep winter, and penguins are huddling together like fuzzy ice cubes.
This date sits exactly 61 days after the summer solstice and 69 days before the fall equinox - it's like the middle child of late summer!
Many fruit trees are dropping their ripest peaches and plums right now, creating a sticky, sweet mess that attracts armies of wasps and bees.
The Perseid meteor shower typically peaks right around August 14, so you might catch shooting stars streaking across the sky like cosmic fireworks.
In northern regions, some maple trees start showing their first tiny hints of red and orange, even though it feels way too early for fall colors.
Ocean temperatures in many places hit their warmest point of the year around mid-August, making the water feel like a giant bathtub.
Grasshoppers and crickets are at their noisiest in mid-August, creating a buzzing symphony that can reach 100 decibels - louder than a garbage disposal!
